exercises.question:
Which separation techniques will you apply for the separation
of the following

subparts.label: a
subparts.text: Sodium chloride from its solution in water

subparts.label: b
subparts.text:
Ammonium chloride from a mixture containing sodium
chloride and ammonium chloride

subparts.label: c
subparts.text: Small pieces of metal in the engine oil of a car

subparts.label: d
subparts.text: Different pigments from an extract of flower petals

subparts.label: j
subparts.text: Fine mud particles suspended in water

exercises.question:
Write the steps you would use for making tea. Use the words
solution, solvent, solute, dissolve, soluble, insoluble, filtrate
and residue.

Pragya tested the solubility of three different substances at
different temperatures and collected the data as given below
(results are given in the following table, as grams of substance
dissolved in 100 grams of water to form a saturated solution).
Temperature in K
Substance Dissolved
Solubility
Potassium nitrate 21 32 62 106 167
Sodium chloride 36 36 36 37 37
Potassium chloride 35 35 40 46 54
Ammonium chloride 24 37 41 55 66

subparts.label: a
subparts.text:
What mass of potassium nitrate would be needed to
produce a saturated solution of potassium nitrate in
50 grams of water at 313 K

subparts.label: b
subparts.text:
Pragya makes a saturated solution of potassium chloride
in water at 353 K and leaves the solution to cool at
room temperature. What would she observe as the
solution cools  Explain.

subparts.label: c
subparts.text:
Find the solubility of each salt at 293 K. Which salt has
the highest solubility at this temperature

subparts.label: d
subparts.text:
What is the effect of change of temperature on the
solubility of a salt

exercises.question:
Classify each of the following as a homogeneous or
heterogeneous mixture.
soda water, wood, air, soil, vinegar, filtered tea.

How would you confirm that a colourless liquid given to you
is pure water
IS MATTER AROUND US PURE  23

Which of the following materials fall in the category of a
pure substance
Identify the solutions among the following mixtures.
Which of the following will show  Tyndall effect
Classify the following into elements, compounds and mixtures.
Which of the following are chemical changes

subparts.label: c
subparts.text: Mixing of iron filings and sand

subparts.label: g
subparts.text:
Burning of a candle
Group Activity
Take an earthen pot (matka), some pebbles and sand. Design a
small-scale filtration plant that you could use to clean
muddy water.
